<USER_REQUEST>
Create the file D:\AI-Business\docs\MODEL_GUIDE.md — comprehensive guide to all AI models.

Platform: AI-powered passive income, RTX 5070 12GB VRAM, 64GB RAM, Windows.

Include ALL:
1. **Model Inventory** (table): name, type, size, location, purpose
2. **Image Generation Models**: SDXL base (coloring pages with LoRA, D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\checkpoints\, 1024x1024), Flux.1 Dev FP8 (wall art, D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\diffusion_models\, FP8 for 12GB)
3. **Text Encoders**: CLIP-L, T5-XXL FP8 at D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\text_encoders\
4. **Upscaler**: 4x-UltraSharp at D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\upscale_models\
5. **LoRAs**: ColoringBook.Redmond (trigger words, weight 0.8-1.0, CFG 7, D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\loras\, Civitai link)
6. **Writing Model**: Qwen 2.5 14B via Ollama (pull command, settings, context window 32K)
7. **LoRA Training Guide**: Kohya_ss on RTX 5070 12GB (fused backward pass, block swapping 20+, FP8 quantization, gradient checkpointing, batch 1, Adafactor/8-bit Adam, 1500-3000 steps, 20-50 images, 64GB RAM helps)
8. **How to Swap/Update Models**: Step-by-step replacement, workflow JSON updates, testing
9. **Recommended LoRAs from Civitai**: Categories (coloring book, watercolor, line art, minimalist, vintage)
10. **VRAM Management**: 12GB sharing, one pipeline at a time, ComfyUI memory settings, --lowvram/--gpu-only flags

After creating the file, send a message to the parent confirming completion.
</USER_REQUEST>

thinking: The user wants me to create a comprehensive MODEL_GUIDE.md file at D:\AI-Business\docs\MODEL_GUIDE.md. Let me create this detailed guide covering all the specified topics.

args.CodeContent:
# 🧠 AI Model Guide — Complete Reference

> **Platform:** AI-Powered Passive Income Business
> **Hardware:** NVIDIA RTX 5070 (12GB VRAM) · 64GB System RAM · Windows
> **Last Updated:** June 2026

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Model Inventory](#1-model-inventory)
2. [Image Generation Models](#2-image-generation-models)
3. [Text Encoders](#3-text-encoders)
4. [Upscaler Models](#4-upscaler-models)
5. [LoRA Adapters](#5-lora-adapters)
6. [Writing Model — Qwen 2.5 14B](#6-writing-model--qwen-25-14b)
7. [LoRA Training Guide (Kohya_ss)](#7-lora-training-guide-kohya_ss)
8. [How to Swap & Update Models](#8-how-to-swap--update-models)
9. [Recommended LoRAs from Civitai](#9-recommended-loras-from-civitai)
10. [VRAM Management](#10-vram-management)

---

## 1. Model Inventory

| # | Model Name | Type | Size (approx.) | Location | Purpose |
|---|-----------|------|-----------------|----------|---------|
| 1 | SDXL Base 1.0 | Checkpoint (Image Gen) | ~6.5 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\checkpoints\` | Coloring page generation (with LoRA) |
| 2 | Flux.1 Dev FP8 | Diffusion Model (Image Gen) | ~8 GB (FP8) | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\diffusion_models\` | Premium wall art & fine art prints |
| 3 | CLIP-L | Text Encoder | ~250 MB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\text_encoders\` | Text-to-image prompt encoding (SDXL & Flux) |
| 4 | T5-XXL FP8 | Text Encoder | ~5 GB (FP8) | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\text_encoders\` | Advanced prompt understanding (Flux) |
| 5 | 4x-UltraSharp | Upscaler (ESRGAN) | ~67 MB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\upscale_models\` | 4× resolution upscaling for print-ready output |
| 6 | ColoringBook.Redmond | LoRA Adapter | ~150 MB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\loras\` | Coloring book style for SDXL |
| 7 | Qwen 2.5 14B | LLM (Text Generation) | ~9 GB (Q4_K_M) | Managed by Ollama | Book descriptions, metadata, SEO copy |

---

## 2. Image Generation Models

### 2.1 SDXL Base 1.0 — Coloring Page Pipeline

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **Model** | Stable Diffusion XL Base 1.0 |
| **File** | `sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors` |
| **Location** | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\checkpoints\` |
| **Native Resolution** | 1024 × 1024 |
| **VRAM Usage** | ~5–6 GB (FP16) |
| **Primary Use** | Coloring book pages (paired with LoRA) |

**How It Works:**
- SDXL generates high-quality base images at 1024×1024 natively.
- When paired with the **ColoringBook.Redmond** LoRA, it produces clean black-and-white line art perfect for coloring books.
- Output is then upscaled via **4x-UltraSharp** for print resolution (4096×4096+).

**Recommended Settings for Coloring Pages:**

```
Resolution:    1024 × 1024
Sampler:       DPM++ 2M Karras
Steps:         25–30
CFG Scale:     7.0
LoRA:          ColoringBook.Redmond @ 0.8–1.0 weight
Negative:      color, shading, gradient, realistic, photograph, blurry
```

**Supported Aspect Ratios (SDXL Optimal):**

| Aspect Ratio | Resolution | Use Case |
|--------------|------------|----------|
| 1:1 | 1024 × 1024 | Square coloring pages |
| 3:4 | 896 × 1152 | Portrait / book pages (8.5×11) |
| 4:3 | 1152 × 896 | Landscape scenes |
| 9:16 | 768 × 1344 | Tall / bookmark designs |
| 16:9 | 1344 × 768 | Wide / banner art |

> [!TIP]
> Always use SDXL-native resolutions (multiples of 64 that total ~1 megapixel). Non-standard resolutions cause artifacts and duplicate subjects.

---

### 2.2 Flux.1 Dev FP8 — Wall Art Pipeline

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **Model** | Flux.1 Dev (FP8 Quantized) |
| **File** | `flux1-dev-fp8.safetensors` |
| **Location** | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\diffusion_models\` |
| **Architecture** | Rectified Flow Transformer |
| **VRAM Usage** | ~10–12 GB (FP8) |
| **Primary Use** | Premium wall art, fine art prints, POD artwork |

**Why FP8?**
- Full Flux.1 Dev is ~24 GB — impossible on 12GB VRAM.
- FP8 quantization reduces to ~8 GB with minimal quality loss.
- Fits within 12GB VRAM alongside text encoders when loaded sequentially.

**How It Works:**
- Flux uses a **Rectified Flow Transformer** architecture (not U-Net like SDXL).
- Requires **two text encoders**: CLIP-L + T5-XXL FP8.
- Produces photorealistic and artistic images with exceptional text rendering.
- Ideal for wall art, canvas prints, and premium POD products.

**Recommended Settings for Wall Art:**

```
Resolution:    1024 × 1024 (or aspect-ratio equivalent)
Sampler:       Euler
Scheduler:     Simple
Steps:         20–30
Guidance:      3.5 (Flux uses lower guidance than SDXL)
```

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Flux does NOT use a negative prompt. Guidance works differently — keep it at 3.0–4.0 for best results. Higher values cause over-saturation.

**Flux vs. SDXL — When to Use Which:**

| Feature | SDXL | Flux.1 Dev FP8 |
|---------|------|----------------|
| Architecture | U-Net | Rectified Flow Transformer |
| Text in Images | Poor | Excellent |
| Photorealism | Good | Exceptional |
| Artistic Styles | Great with LoRAs | Great natively |
| LoRA Ecosystem | Massive | Growing |
| VRAM Usage | ~6 GB | ~10–12 GB |
| Speed (RTX 5070) | ~15–25 sec | ~30–60 sec |
| **Best For** | Coloring pages, LoRA styles | Wall art, fine art, text-heavy |

---

## 3. Text Encoders

Text encoders convert your text prompts into numerical representations (embeddings) that the image model can understand.

### 3.1 CLIP-L (OpenAI CLIP ViT-L/14)

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **File** | `clip_l.safetensors` |
| **Location** | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\text_encoders\` |
| **Size** | ~250 MB |
| **Used By** | SDXL (primary encoder), Flux.1 (first encoder) |
| **Token Limit** | 77 tokens |

- Standard CLIP encoder used across most Stable Diffusion models.
- Handles the "big picture" semantic meaning of prompts.
- Shared between SDXL and Flux pipelines — no need for duplicates.

### 3.2 T5-XXL FP8

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **File** | `t5xxl_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors` |
| **Location** | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\text_encoders\` |
| **Size** | ~5 GB (FP8) / ~10 GB (FP16) |
| **Used By** | Flux.1 Dev (second encoder) |
| **Token Limit** | 256+ tokens |

- Google's T5-XXL provides deep language understanding for Flux.
- FP8 version cuts VRAM usage in half with negligible quality impact.
- Enables Flux's superior text rendering and complex prompt comprehension.

> [!NOTE]
> The FP8 version (`t5xxl_fp8_e4m3fn`) is essential for 12GB VRAM. The full FP16 version would consume 10 GB VRAM just for this one encoder, leaving nothing for the actual diffusion model.

**Loading Strategy for 12GB VRAM:**
```
1. Load CLIP-L        → ~250 MB VRAM
2. Load T5-XXL FP8    → ~5 GB VRAM
3. Encode prompt       → Generate embeddings
4. Unload encoders     → Free VRAM
5. Load Flux.1 FP8    → ~8 GB VRAM
6. Generate image      → Using cached embeddings
```

ComfyUI handles this automatically with its intelligent memory management.

---

## 4. Upscaler Models

### 4x-UltraSharp (ESRGAN)

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **File** | `4x-UltraSharp.pth` |
| **Location** | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\upscale_models\` |
| **Scale Factor** | 4× |
| **VRAM Usage** | ~200 MB |
| **Primary Use** | Print-ready resolution upscaling |

**Why 4x-UltraSharp?**
- Industry-standard ESRGAN upscaler known for preserving fine details.
- Perfect for line art and coloring pages — keeps lines crisp and clean.
- Minimal VRAM footprint, can run alongside generation models.

**Upscaling Pipeline:**

```
Source (1024 × 1024) → 4x-UltraSharp → Output (4096 × 4096)
                                        = 300 DPI at ~13.6" × 13.6"
```

**Print Resolution Reference:**

| Source Resolution | After 4× Upscale | Print Size @ 300 DPI |
|-------------------|-------------------|----------------------|
| 1024 × 1024 | 4096 × 4096 | 13.6" × 13.6" |
| 896 × 1152 | 3584 × 4608 | 11.9" × 15.4" (≈ Letter) |
| 1152 × 896 | 4608 × 3584 | 15.4" × 11.9" (≈ Landscape) |
| 768 × 1344 | 3072 × 5376 | 10.2" × 17.9" |

> [!TIP]
> For Amazon KDP coloring books (8.5" × 11"), generate at **896 × 1152** then upscale to **3584 × 4608** for perfect 300+ DPI output.

---

## 5. LoRA Adapters

### 5.1 ColoringBook.Redmond

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **Full Name** | ColoringBook.Redmond - Coloring Book Style |
| **File** | `ColoringBookRedmond.safetensors` (or similar) |
| **Location** | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\loras\` |
| **Base Model** | SDXL 1.0 |
| **Civitai** | [https://civitai.com/models/131362](https://civitai.com/models/131362) |

**Trigger Words:**
```
ColoringBookAF
```

> [!IMPORTANT]
> You **must** include the trigger word `ColoringBookAF` in your prompt for the LoRA to activate. Without it, the LoRA will have minimal effect regardless of weight.

**Recommended Settings:**

| Setting | Value | Notes |
|---------|-------|-------|
| **LoRA Weight** | 0.8 – 1.0 | Start at 0.8, increase for stronger effect |
| **CFG Scale** | 7.0 | Standard for SDXL |
| **Sampler** | DPM++ 2M Karras | Best balance of quality/speed |
| **Steps** | 25–30 | More steps = cleaner lines |

**Example Prompt (Coloring Page):**
```
ColoringBookAF, a majestic lion standing proudly, intricate mane details,
clean black outlines on white background, coloring book page,
no shading, no color fill, high detail line art
```

**Example Negative Prompt:**
```
color, colored, shading, gradient, realistic, photograph, blurry,
low quality, watermark, text, grayscale fill, smudged lines
```

**Weight Tuning Guide:**

| Weight | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| 0.6 | Subtle line art influence, some shading may remain |
| 0.8 | **Recommended starting point** — clean lines, minimal shading |
| 1.0 | Strong coloring book style, very clean black/white |
| 1.2 | Over-stylized, lines may become too thick or abstract |

> [!WARNING]
> LoRA weights above 1.2 can cause artifacts, oversaturated lines, or anatomical distortions. Stay within the 0.8–1.0 range for production work.

---

## 6. Writing Model — Qwen 2.5 14B

### Overview

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **Model** | Qwen 2.5 14B |
| **Quantization** | Q4_K_M (via Ollama) |
| **Size on Disk** | ~9 GB |
| **RAM Usage** | ~10–12 GB |
| **Context Window** | 32,768 tokens (32K) |
| **Served By** | Ollama (local inference) |

### Installation & Setup

**Step 1: Install Ollama**
Download from [https://ollama.com](https://ollama.com) and install on Windows.

**Step 2: Pull the Model**
```powershell
ollama pull qwen2.5:14b
```

**Step 3: Verify Installation**
```powershell
ollama list
# Should show: qwen2.5:14b    ~9 GB
```

**Step 4: Test the Model**
```powershell
ollama run qwen2.5:14b "Write a one-paragraph book description for a Christian coloring book about Psalms."
```

### Configuration & Settings

**Ollama Modelfile (Custom Settings):**
```
FROM qwen2.5:14b

PARAMETER temperature 0.7
PARAMETER top_p 0.9
PARAMETER top_k 40
PARAMETER num_ctx 32768
PARAMETER repeat_penalty 1.1
PARAMETER num_predict 2048

SYSTEM """You are an expert copywriter specializing in Christian publishing,
Amazon KDP book descriptions, and SEO-optimized product metadata.
Write compelling, faith-based content that resonates with Christian audiences."""
```

**Create the custom model:**
```powershell
ollama create christian-writer -f .\Modelfile
ollama run christian-writer
```

### Use Cases

| Task | Temperature | Max Tokens | Notes |
|------|-------------|------------|-------|
| Book descriptions | 0.7 | 500–800 | Compelling, emotional |
| KDP keywords/tags | 0.3 | 200 | Precise, SEO-focused |
| Chapter titles | 0.8 | 100 | Creative variety |
| Social media posts | 0.8 | 300 | Engaging, shareable |
| Prompt engineering | 0.5 | 200 | Consistent, technical |
| Blog/devotional content | 0.7 | 1500 | Natural, flowing |

### API Access

Ollama runs a local API server on `http://localhost:11434`:

```powershell
# Generate text via API
curl http://localhost:11434/api/generate -d '{
  "model": "qwen2.5:14b",
  "prompt": "Write 7 Amazon keywords for a Christian coloring book about Psalms",
  "stream": false,
  "options": {
    "temperature": 0.3,
    "num_ctx": 32768
  }
}'
```

**Python Integration:**
```python
import requests

def generate_text(prompt, temperature=0.7, max_tokens=1024):
    response = requests.post("http://localhost:11434/api/generate", json={
        "model": "qwen2.5:14b",
        "prompt": prompt,
        "stream": False,
        "options": {
            "temperature": temperature,
            "num_predict": max_tokens,
            "num_ctx": 32768
        }
    })
    return response.json()["response"]
```

> [!NOTE]
> Qwen 2.5 14B runs entirely on **system RAM** (not VRAM by default with Ollama), so it can run simultaneously with ComfyUI image generation. Your 64GB RAM easily accommodates both.

---

## 7. LoRA Training Guide (Kohya_ss)

### Hardware Profile for Training

| Resource | Available | Training Impact |
|----------|-----------|-----------------|
| GPU | RTX 5070, 12 GB VRAM | Tight — requires aggressive memory optimization |
| RAM | 64 GB | Excellent — enables large dataset caching & block swapping |
| Storage | SSD recommended | Faster checkpoint saves & data loading |

### Pre-Training Checklist

1. **Install Kohya_ss GUI**: [https://github.com/bmaltais/kohya_ss](https://github.com/bmaltais/kohya_ss)
2. **Install CUDA 12.x** matching your RTX 5070 driver
3. **Prepare dataset**: 20–50 high-quality training images
4. **Create captions**: Use BLIP/WD14 auto-captioning, then manually refine
5. **Set regularization images**: Optional but recommended (200–500 class images)

### Critical Memory Optimization Settings (12GB VRAM)

> [!CAUTION]
> Without these settings, training WILL crash with out-of-memory errors on 12GB VRAM. Apply ALL of the following:

```yaml
# === REQUIRED FOR 12GB VRAM ===

# 1. Fused Backward Pass
#    Fuses the optimizer step with the backward pass to reduce peak VRAM.
#    Saves ~2-3 GB VRAM.
fused_backward_pass: true

# 2. Block Swapping (20+ blocks)
#    Offloads model blocks to system RAM during training.
#    Your 64GB RAM makes this very effective.
#    Higher = more RAM used but less VRAM needed.
blocks_to_swap: 20     # Minimum 20 for 12GB; try 24-28 if still OOM

# 3. FP8 Base Model Quantization
#    Quantizes the base model weights to FP8 during training.
#    Halves the VRAM used by frozen model weights.
fp8_base: true

# 4. Gradient Checkpointing
#    Trades compute time for VRAM by recomputing activations.
#    Essential for any GPU under 16GB.
gradient_checkpointing: true

# 5. Batch Size = 1
#    Minimum batch size. Larger batches multiply VRAM usage.
train_batch_size: 1
```

### Recommended Training Configuration

```yaml
# === MODEL ===
pretrained_model: "D:\\ComfyUI\\ComfyUI\\models\\checkpoints\\sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors"
model_type: "sdxl"

# === DATASET ===
train_data_dir: "D:\\AI-Business\\training\\datasets\\my_lora\\img"
resolution: 1024
caption_extension: ".txt"
shuffle_caption: true
keep_tokens: 1               # Keep first token (trigger word) fixed

# === TRAINING PARAMS ===
train_batch_size: 1
num_epochs: 10                # Or use max_train_steps
max_train_steps: 1500         # 1500–3000 depending on dataset size
learning_rate: 1e-4           # For Adafactor, or 1e-4 for 8-bit Adam
lr_scheduler: "cosine"
lr_warmup_ratio: 0.05

# === OPTIMIZER ===
# Option A: Adafactor (recommended — lower VRAM)
optimizer_type: "adafactor"
optimizer_args:
  - "scale_parameter=False"
  - "relative_step=False"
  - "warmup_init=False"

# Option B: 8-bit Adam (alternative — good balance)
# optimizer_type: "AdamW8bit"

# === NETWORK (LoRA) ===
network_module: "networks.lora"
network_dim: 32               # Rank — 16-64 typical
network_alpha: 16              # Usually dim/2
network_train_unet_only: false # Train text encoder too for style LoRAs

# === MEMORY OPTIMIZATION (12GB VRAM) ===
mixed_precision: "fp16"
full_fp16: false
fp8_base: true
gradient_checkpointing: true
fused_backward_pass: true
blocks_to_swap: 20
train_batch_size: 1

# === OUTPUT ===
output_dir: "D:\\AI-Business\\training\\output"
output_name: "my_custom_lora"
save_every_n_steps: 500
save_model_as: "safetensors"
```

### Dataset Preparation Guidelines

| Parameter | Recommendation |
|-----------|---------------|
| **Image Count** | 20–50 images (sweet spot for style LoRAs) |
| **Image Quality** | High-res, consistent style, clean backgrounds |
| **Image Format** | PNG preferred, no compression artifacts |
| **Resolution** | 1024×1024 or higher (will be bucketed) |
| **Captions** | Descriptive, include trigger word first |
| **Repeats** | Adjust so `repeats × images ≈ 200–500 steps/epoch` |

**Caption Format Example:**
```
ColoringBookCustom, a detailed butterfly with symmetrical wings,
intricate patterns, clean line art, black outlines on white background
```

### Training Steps Guide

| Dataset Size | Recommended Steps | Epochs (approx.) |
|-------------|-------------------|-------------------|
| 20 images | 1,500 – 2,000 | 10–15 |
| 30 images | 2,000 – 2,500 | 8–12 |
| 50 images | 2,500 – 3,000 | 6–10 |

### Step-by-Step Training Process

```
1. Launch Kohya_ss GUI
2. Select "LoRA" training tab
3. Load your base model (SDXL checkpoint)
4. Set dataset folder with proper structure:
   D:\AI-Business\training\datasets\my_lora\
   └── img\
       └── 10_trigger_word class\    ← "10" = repeats
           ├── image_001.png
           ├── image_001.txt          ← caption
           ├── image_002.png
           ├── image_002.txt
           └── ...
5. Configure ALL memory optimization settings above
6. Set learning rate, steps, and optimizer
7. Start training
8. Monitor for OOM — if it occurs, increase blocks_to_swap to 24+
9. Test intermediate checkpoints (saved every 500 steps)
10. Pick the best checkpoint by visual comparison
```

> [!TIP]
> Your **64GB system RAM** is a huge advantage for LoRA training. It allows aggressive block swapping (20–28 blocks) without any system memory pressure. Most users with 16–32GB RAM can't swap as many blocks.

---

## 8. How to Swap & Update Models

### 8.1 Replacing a Checkpoint Model

**Step 1: Download the new model**
```powershell
# Place the new checkpoint in the checkpoints directory
# Example: Replacing SDXL Base with a fine-tuned variant
Move-Item "C:\Users\joel0\Downloads\new_model.safetensors" `
    "D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\checkpoints\new_model.safetensors"
```

**Step 2: Update your ComfyUI workflow**
1. Open your workflow JSON file in a text editor.
2. Find the `CheckpointLoaderSimple` node.
3. Change the `ckpt_name` value:

```diff
 {
   "class_type": "CheckpointLoaderSimple",
   "inputs": {
-    "ckpt_name": "sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors"
+    "ckpt_name": "new_model.safetensors"
   }
 }
```

4. Save the workflow JSON.

**Step 3: Test the new model**
```
1. Launch ComfyUI
2. Load the updated workflow
3. Generate a test image with your standard prompt
4. Compare quality against the previous model
5. Check VRAM usage — ensure it fits in 12GB
6. If satisfied, update all production workflows
```

### 8.2 Replacing a LoRA

```powershell
# 1. Download new LoRA to the loras directory
Move-Item "C:\Users\joel0\Downloads\NewLoRA.safetensors" `
    "D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\loras\NewLoRA.safetensors"
```

Update the `LoraLoader` node in your workflow JSON:
```diff
 {
   "class_type": "LoraLoader",
   "inputs": {
-    "lora_name": "ColoringBookRedmond.safetensors",
-    "strength_model": 0.8,
-    "strength_clip": 0.8
+    "lora_name": "NewLoRA.safetensors",
+    "strength_model": 1.0,
+    "strength_clip": 1.0
   }
 }
```

> [!WARNING]
> Always check the new LoRA's recommended weight and trigger words on its Civitai page before deploying. Using the wrong settings can produce poor results.

### 8.3 Replacing an Upscaler

```powershell
# 1. Download new upscaler
Move-Item "C:\Users\joel0\Downloads\4x-NewUpscaler.pth" `
    "D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\upscale_models\4x-NewUpscaler.pth"
```

Update the `UpscaleModelLoader` node:
```diff
 {
   "class_type": "UpscaleModelLoader",
   "inputs": {
-    "model_name": "4x-UltraSharp.pth"
+    "model_name": "4x-NewUpscaler.pth"
   }
 }
```

### 8.4 Updating Ollama Models

```powershell
# Update to latest version of Qwen 2.5 14B
ollama pull qwen2.5:14b

# Or switch to a different model entirely
ollama pull llama3:8b

# Remove old models to free disk space
ollama rm old-model-name

# List all installed models
ollama list
```

### 8.5 General Model Swap Checklist

- [ ] Download new model to the correct directory
- [ ] Verify file integrity (check file size matches source)
- [ ] Update workflow JSON with new filename
- [ ] Update any trigger words / prompt templates
- [ ] Adjust weights / CFG / settings per model recommendations
- [ ] Generate 5–10 test images with standard prompts
- [ ] Compare output quality against previous model
- [ ] Check VRAM usage fits within 12GB
- [ ] Update this MODEL_GUIDE.md with new model details
- [ ] Commit workflow changes to version control

---

## 9. Recommended LoRAs from Civitai

Browse and download from [https://civitai.com](https://civitai.com). All LoRAs below are compatible with **SDXL 1.0**.

### 📚 Coloring Book Style

| LoRA Name | Civitai Link | Trigger Word | Recommended Weight | Notes |
|-----------|-------------|--------------|-------------------|-------|
| ColoringBook.Redmond | [civitai.com/models/131362](https://civitai.com/models/131362) | `ColoringBookAF` | 0.8–1.0 | **Currently using** — excellent clean lines |
| Coloring Book Style | Search "coloring book SDXL" | Varies | 0.7–1.0 | Alternative option, compare outputs |
| Kids Coloring Book | Search "kids coloring SDXL" | Varies | 0.8–1.0 | Simpler, child-friendly designs |

### 🎨 Watercolor Style

| LoRA Name | Search Term | Trigger Word | Recommended Weight | Notes |
|-----------|------------|--------------|-------------------|-------|
| Watercolor Style SDXL | "watercolor SDXL LoRA" | Varies | 0.6–0.9 | Beautiful for wall art prints |
| Aquarelle Watercolor | "aquarelle SDXL" | Varies | 0.7–1.0 | Soft, artistic watercolor effect |

### ✏️ Line Art Style

| LoRA Name | Search Term | Trigger Word | Recommended Weight | Notes |
|-----------|------------|--------------|-------------------|-------|
| Line Art Style | "line art SDXL LoRA" | Varies | 0.7–1.0 | Clean illustration lines |
| Ink Drawing | "ink drawing SDXL" | Varies | 0.6–0.9 | Bold ink-style line work |
| Pen Sketch | "pen sketch SDXL" | Varies | 0.7–1.0 | Hand-drawn sketch aesthetic |

### 🏛️ Minimalist Style

| LoRA Name | Search Term | Trigger Word | Recommended Weight | Notes |
|-----------|------------|--------------|-------------------|-------|
| Minimalist Illustration | "minimalist SDXL LoRA" | Varies | 0.6–0.8 | Clean, modern minimalism |
| Flat Design | "flat design SDXL" | Varies | 0.7–1.0 | Flat vector-style illustrations |
| Simple Shapes | "simple geometric SDXL" | Varies | 0.5–0.8 | Geometric minimalist art |

### 🕰️ Vintage / Retro Style

| LoRA Name | Search Term | Trigger Word | Recommended Weight | Notes |
|-----------|------------|--------------|-------------------|-------|
| Vintage Illustration | "vintage illustration SDXL" | Varies | 0.6–0.9 | Classic book illustration feel |
| Retro Poster | "retro poster SDXL" | Varies | 0.7–1.0 | Mid-century poster aesthetic |
| Antique Engraving | "engraving SDXL LoRA" | Varies | 0.5–0.8 | Classic etching/engraving style |

> [!TIP]
> **How to evaluate a LoRA on Civitai:**
> 1. Check the **download count** and **rating** — popular LoRAs are usually reliable.
> 2. Review the **example images** — do they match your desired style?
> 3. Read the **description** for trigger words and recommended settings.
> 4. Verify **base model compatibility** — must say "SDXL 1.0" or "Flux" as applicable.
> 5. Check the **file size** — typical LoRAs are 50–300 MB.
> 6. Download and test with 5–10 prompts before committing to production.

---

## 10. VRAM Management

### 10.1 Understanding Your 12GB VRAM Budget

The RTX 5070's 12GB VRAM is shared between:
- **Windows Desktop** (~500 MB baseline)
- **AI Model Weights** (main consumer)
- **Activations & Intermediate Tensors** (during generation)
- **Any other GPU-accelerated apps** (browsers, video players, etc.)

**Effective Available VRAM: ~11–11.5 GB**

### 10.2 VRAM Usage by Pipeline

| Pipeline | Components Loaded | Peak VRAM | Status |
|----------|-------------------|-----------|--------|
| SDXL + LoRA | Checkpoint + LoRA + VAE | ~6.5 GB | ✅ Comfortable |
| SDXL + LoRA + Upscale | Above + ESRGAN | ~7 GB | ✅ Comfortable |
| Flux.1 FP8 (sequential) | T5+CLIP → Flux → VAE | ~10 GB | ⚠️ Tight but works |
| Flux.1 FP8 (all at once) | Everything loaded | ~14 GB | ❌ OOM — use sequential |
| LoRA Training (optimized) | Base + LoRA + Optimizer | ~10–11 GB | ⚠️ Requires all optimizations |

### 10.3 The Golden Rule: One Pipeline at a Time

> [!CAUTION]
> **NEVER run ComfyUI image generation and LoRA training simultaneously.** Each consumes 6–11 GB VRAM. Running both WILL crash your system.

**Safe Concurrent Operations:**

| Process A | Process B | Safe? | Notes |
|-----------|-----------|-------|-------|
| ComfyUI (SDXL) | Ollama (Qwen 2.5) | ✅ Yes | Ollama uses RAM, not VRAM |
| ComfyUI (Flux) | Ollama (Qwen 2.5) | ✅ Yes | Same — Ollama is RAM-only |
| ComfyUI (SDXL) | ComfyUI (Flux) | ❌ No | Can't run two GPU pipelines |
| ComfyUI (any) | Kohya Training | ❌ No | Both need full GPU |
| Kohya Training | Ollama (Qwen 2.5) | ✅ Yes | Training = GPU, Ollama = RAM |

### 10.4 ComfyUI Memory Settings

**Launch Flags (add to your ComfyUI launch shortcut or script):**

```powershell
# Option 1: Low VRAM mode (aggressive offloading)
# Use when running Flux or experiencing OOM errors
python main.py --lowvram

# Option 2: GPU Only mode (keep everything on GPU)
# Use when running SDXL and you have enough VRAM headroom
python main.py --gpu-only

# Option 3: Default (recommended for most workflows)
# ComfyUI auto-manages memory with intelligent caching
python main.py
```

**Flag Reference:**

| Flag | Effect | When to Use |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `--lowvram` | Aggressively offloads to RAM, loads model pieces as needed | Flux.1 FP8 workflows, OOM errors |
| `--gpu-only` | Keeps all models in VRAM, fastest inference | SDXL-only workflows with headroom |
| `--highvram` | Never offloads anything | DO NOT USE on 12GB — will OOM |
| (default) | Smart auto-management | General use — recommended starting point |
| `--cpu` | Runs entirely on CPU | Emergency fallback only — very slow |

### 10.5 Memory Optimization Tips

**Before Generating:**
```
1. Close Chrome/Edge (browsers use 200-500 MB VRAM for GPU acceleration)
2. Close any games or GPU-accelerated apps
3. If switching from Flux to SDXL, restart ComfyUI to clear VRAM
```

**ComfyUI-Specific Tips:**
```
1. Use "Unload Models" node between different model pipelines
2. Enable "Smart Memory" in ComfyUI settings (on by default)
3. For Flux: use sequential loading — encode text first, then load diffusion model
4. Clear VRAM between runs: Ctrl+Shift+Delete in ComfyUI (if available)
5. Monitor VRAM: use `nvidia-smi` in terminal or Task Manager → Performance → GPU
```

**Monitor VRAM in Real-Time:**
```powershell
# One-time check
nvidia-smi

# Continuous monitoring (updates every 2 seconds)
nvidia-smi -l 2

# Just VRAM usage
nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used,memory.total --format=csv
```

### 10.6 Troubleshooting OOM (Out of Memory) Errors

| Symptom | Cause | Fix |
|---------|-------|-----|
| CUDA OOM during SDXL generation | Too many models loaded | Restart ComfyUI, close other GPU apps |
| CUDA OOM during Flux generation | Encoders + model loaded simultaneously | Use `--lowvram` flag, use sequential loading |
| CUDA OOM during upscaling | Large image + model in memory | Add "Unload Model" node before upscaler |
| CUDA OOM during LoRA training | Missing optimization settings | Enable ALL settings in Section 7 |
| System freeze / black screen | Total GPU memory exhaustion | Hard reboot, then reduce batch size or add `--lowvram` |
| Slow generation but no OOM | Excessive RAM offloading | Check if `--lowvram` is set; try default mode instead |

> [!TIP]
> **Quick VRAM Reset:** If ComfyUI is holding onto VRAM from a previous workflow, the fastest fix is to restart ComfyUI entirely. This releases all GPU memory cleanly.

---

## Appendix A: Model Download Links

| Model | Download Source | Direct Link |
|-------|----------------|-------------|
| SDXL Base 1.0 | Hugging Face | [stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-base-1.0](https://huggingface.co/stabilityai/stable-diffusion-xl-base-1.0) |
| Flux.1 Dev | Hugging Face | [black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-dev](https://huggingface.co/black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-dev) |
| Flux.1 Dev FP8 | Hugging Face | Search "flux1-dev-fp8" on Hugging Face |
| CLIP-L | Hugging Face | Bundled with ComfyUI or SDXL downloads |
| T5-XXL FP8 | Hugging Face | Search "t5xxl_fp8_e4m3fn" on Hugging Face |
| 4x-UltraSharp | OpenModelDB | [openmodeldb.info](https://openmodeldb.info/models/4x-UltraSharp) |
| ColoringBook.Redmond | Civitai | [civitai.com/models/131362](https://civitai.com/models/131362) |
| Qwen 2.5 14B | Ollama | `ollama pull qwen2.5:14b` |
| Kohya_ss | GitHub | [github.com/bmaltais/kohya_ss](https://github.com/bmaltais/kohya_ss) |
| Ollama | Official Site | [ollama.com](https://ollama.com) |

---

## Appendix B: Directory Map

```
D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\
├── checkpoints\
│   └── sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors        ← SDXL Base
├── diffusion_models\
│   └── flux1-dev-fp8.safetensors          ← Flux.1 Dev FP8
├── text_encoders\
│   ├── clip_l.safetensors                 ← CLIP-L
│   └── t5xxl_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors       ← T5-XXL FP8
├── upscale_models\
│   └── 4x-UltraSharp.pth                 ← ESRGAN Upscaler
├── loras\
│   └── ColoringBookRedmond.safetensors    ← Coloring Book LoRA
└── vae\
    └── (bundled with checkpoints)

D:\AI-Business\training\
├── datasets\                              ← Training image datasets
│   └── my_lora\
│       └── img\
│           └── 10_trigger class\
├── output\                                ← Trained LoRA output
└── configs\                               ← Training config files
```
